task: I’m putting together a short intro for a project handoff and need it to cover the essentials without dragging on. What’s a straightforward way to open it so the next person has the key details right away?

turns.agent_response:
Use a short opener like this:

> **Project Handoff**
> **Project:** [name]
> **Location:** [site]
> **Status:** [current phase]
> **What’s complete:** [brief list]
> **What’s open:** [brief list]
> **Next step:** [what happens next]
> **Key issues:** [hold points / delays / risks]
> **Owner of next action:** [name]

### If you want it even tighter:
> **Handoff summary:** This job is at [status]. Completed: [X]. Open items: [Y]. Next step is [Z]. Main hold point is [A]. [Name] owns the next action.

### Keep it clean
- no long story
- no blame
- no side notes
- just the facts someone needs to pick it up

If you want, I can turn that into a **one-paragraph handoff intro** or a **fill-in template**.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Clean enough. I’d use the short opener and keep it like that — project, status, open items, next step, hold point, who’s on it. No fluff.
